mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth accelerates month-over-month, signaling robust labor demand and consumer income expansion. This drives near-term equity valuations upward as forward earnings expectations improve, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:April 2010 labor market recovery phase post-financial crisis
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market stabilization
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive PAYEMS growth fails to produce positive 20d returns in > 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate remains elevated above 4.5% while inflation (CPIAUCSL) shows persistent month-over-month growth, indicating monetary policy is restrictive relative to inflation dynamics. This constrains equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as real rates remain tight.
evidence_refs: fact:April 2010 Fed maintaining restrictive stance post-crisis
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with above-trend inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite tight monetary conditions, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline month-over-month by more than 5%, signaling weakening residential construction demand and forward-looking consumer confidence deterioration. This negative demand shock propagates to equity valuations within 5 trading days as market participants reprrice growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing sector volatility in 2010 recovery period
regime_hint: post-crisis demand recovery with housing sector fragility
failure_condition: Negative HOUST shocks fail to produce negative 5d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) accelerates above its 3-month moving average while unemployment (UNRATE) remains stable or declines, indicating supply-side capacity utilization is rising without labor market slack. This signals sustainable earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO > 3-month MA AND UNRATE month-over-month change <= 0
evidence_refs: fact:April 2010 industrial recovery with stable unemployment
failure_condition: Joint INDPRO acceleration and stable UNRATE fail to produce positive 20d returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or interaction Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) decline month-over-month while housing starts (HOUST) also contract, indicating synchronized demand weakness across consumer and residential sectors. This dual demand shock produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as forward earnings revisions turn negative.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month decline > 1% AND HOUST month-over-month decline > 3%
evidence_refs: fact:Demand sector correlation in 2010 recovery uncertainty
failure_condition: Synchronized RSAFS and HOUST declines fail to produce negative 60d returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or interaction Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, signaling labor market deterioration and forward earnings risk. This triggers equity repricing downward, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as recession risk premia increase.
evidence_refs: fact:Labor market volatility in 2010 post-crisis environment
failure_condition: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months but SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate declines below 2% while real GDP growth (GDPC1) remains positive quarter-over-quarter, indicating monetary accommodation is supporting demand without deflationary pressure. This liquidity-driven environment produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as equity risk premia compress.
evidence_refs: fact:April 2010 accommodative Fed policy supporting recovery
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with positive real growth
failure_condition: Accommodative monetary conditions with positive growth fail to produce positive 60d returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or interaction Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month while payroll employment (PAYEMS) also contracts, indicating synchronized supply-side and labor market weakness. This signals earnings recession risk and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as market reprices growth expectations sharply downward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month decline > 0.5% AND PAYEMS month-over-month decline > 0.1%
regime_hint: supply-side contraction with labor market weakness
failure_condition: Synchronized INDPRO and PAYEMS declines fail to produce negative 5d returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or interaction Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
